cases.headline:
Real outcomes.
Measurable results.
cases.sub: Every engagement is built around a specific operational problem. Here is what our clients achieved.
cases.ctaSub: Start with a 30-day diagnostic. We map your operation and identify the highest-value changes.

ecommerce-wms-peak-readiness.headline: WMS Implementation Cuts Picking Errors 35% Before Peak Season
ecommerce-wms-peak-readiness.summary: A fast-growing online retailer with three fulfilment centres needed to handle a 4× volume surge during peak. We designed and deployed a WMS solution that delivered operational control in 90 days.

wholesale-tms-freight-savings.headline: TMS Rollout Reduces Freight Spend by 22% Across 200+ Daily Routes
wholesale-tms-freight-savings.summary: A regional distributor running 200+ daily routes had no visibility into carrier performance or route costs. Our TMS implementation unified their carrier base and introduced dynamic route optimisation.

omnichannel-inventory-visibility.headline: Real-Time Inventory Visibility Eliminates Stockouts Across 5 Sales Channels
omnichannel-inventory-visibility.summary: A consumer brand selling across its own website, two marketplaces, and two retail partners had chronic overselling and stockout problems. A unified inventory layer solved both in one engagement.

manufacturing-warehouse-optimisation.headline: Warehouse Redesign Recovers 32% Floor Space Without Capital Expenditure
manufacturing-warehouse-optimisation.summary: A mid-size manufacturer was paying for off-site storage due to perceived space constraints. A systematic warehouse analysis revealed significant recoverable capacity through slotting and flow redesign.

wholesale-distribution-network.headline: Distribution Network Consolidation Delivers $1.4M Annual Savings
wholesale-distribution-network.summary: Operating six regional warehouses with overlapping coverage zones, a building materials wholesaler was duplicating inventory and freight costs. A network rationalisation study identified a 3-warehouse model with better reach.

ecommerce-returns-processing.headline: Returns Processing Time Halved, Recovery Rate Reaches 87%
ecommerce-returns-processing.summary: A fashion platform with a 28% return rate was losing margin on every return due to slow grading and restocking. A dedicated returns cell with defined grading logic transformed returns from a cost centre into a recovery engine.

wholesale-erp-misfit-rescue.headline: Enterprise ERP Misfit Identified and Replaced, Eliminating 400+ Monthly Order Errors
wholesale-erp-misfit-rescue.summary: A Mississauga food wholesaler with 1,200+ SKUs was sold an enterprise retail ERP built for chains ten times their size. After six months of cascading failures, we diagnosed the root cause and delivered a right-sized replacement in 11 weeks.

wholesale-b2b-pricing-recovery.headline: B2B Pricing Engine Rebuilt After System Migration — Invoice Disputes Down 94%
wholesale-b2b-pricing-recovery.summary: After a platform migration, a Burnaby specialty food distributor's customer portal was showing incorrect prices across 650+ restaurant accounts. Staff were manually correcting 80+ invoices daily. We rebuilt the pricing configuration without a system replacement.

wholesale-systems-integration.headline: Disconnected Shopify, ERP, and Accounting Systems Unified — Manual Re-Entry Eliminated
wholesale-systems-integration.summary: A Richmond Hill Asian food importer was re-entering every B2B order manually across three disconnected systems. We designed and built the integration layer that connected Shopify B2B, their ERP, and Xero — cutting order processing time by 71%.

wholesale-erp-upgrade-failure-rescue.headline: ERP Upgrade Go-Live Collapsed, Rescued and Completed — Operations Restored Within 18 Days
wholesale-erp-upgrade-failure-rescue.summary: A Hamilton food wholesaler's long-planned ERP upgrade collapsed on go-live day: an incomplete data migration left 2,800 customer records unreadable, order processing halted for 72 hours, and the implementation partner withdrew. We stabilised operations, recovered the data, and completed the upgrade the original team could not.

caseDetail.readySub: Start with a 30-day diagnostic. We map your operation and identify the highest-value changes before any system selection begins.

warehouse.headline: Warehouse management that delivers measurable results in 90 days
warehouse.sub: We design, select, and implement warehouse management systems matched to your operation — not the vendor's default configuration. Every engagement is anchored to a measurable first outcome within 90 days.

services.title: Inventory Optimisation

Omnichannel inventory master layer with ATP buffers

Cycle count programme design replacing full physical counts

services.title: Returns & Special Operations

Grading decision matrices per product category
Grade B liquidation channel setup
Inbound receiving and quality control process redesign

ecommerce.headline: Operational infrastructure for e-commerce at scale
ecommerce.sub: From single-warehouse DTC brands to multi-channel platforms, we design and implement the WMS and TMS systems that keep fulfilment accurate and freight costs under control.
ecommerce.challengesHeadline: The operational gaps that cost e-commerce operators margin
ecommerce.challengesPara: E-commerce logistics combines the complexity of retail velocity with the precision demands of individual order fulfilment. The gaps between channels, systems, and physical operations compound quickly — especially at scale.

challenges.title: Peak volume without infrastructure
challenges.desc: 4× volume surges during peak season with WMS systems that weren't designed for it — leading to errors, overtime, and backorders.

challenges.title: Multi-channel inventory desynchronisation
challenges.desc: Inventory counts that live in 5 different systems, updated on different schedules, leading to overselling and excess safety stock.

challenges.title: Returns destroying margin
challenges.desc: 28%+ return rates with no defined grading process — slow restocking, inconsistent decisions, and written-off sellable stock.

challenges.title: Carrier cost and SLA opacity
challenges.desc: No visibility into which carriers are underperforming, and no leverage to renegotiate — freight spend growing faster than revenue.

solutions.title: WMS Implementation
solutions.desc: End-to-end warehouse management system design, selection, and deployment — including directed put-away, pick wave logic, and labour productivity tracking.

solutions.title: Omnichannel Inventory
solutions.desc: Unified inventory master layer across all sales channels with channel-level ATP buffers and automated daily reconciliation.

solutions.title: Returns Processing
solutions.desc: Dedicated returns cell design with grading decision matrices, 48-hour processing SLAs, and Grade B liquidation channel setup.

solutions.title: Carrier & TMS Strategy
solutions.desc: Carrier network audit, performance benchmarking, TMS implementation with dynamic route optimisation for last-mile delivery.

about.headline: Operators who've been in the warehouse.
about.sub: We're not strategy consultants who hand over a report and leave. We're operations practitioners who've run warehouses, managed carrier networks, and implemented the systems ourselves.
about.ctaSub: We take on a limited number of engagements each quarter to ensure every client gets senior-level attention.

contact.headline: Start a conversation.
contact.sub: Whether you're dealing with a specific operational problem or just want to understand what a diagnostic looks like — we're happy to talk.
contact.formSuccess: Message sent. We'll be in touch within one business day.
contact.responseDesc: We respond to all enquiries within one business day.
contact.engagementsDesc: We take on a limited number of new engagements each quarter.

advisory.headline: A thinking partner for decisions that compound.
advisory.sub: Senior strategic counsel delivered through a monthly retainer — keeping your leadership team honest, focused, and accountable to the decisions that shape your trajectory.
advisory.whoForPara1: Founders and CEOs of $10M–$100M businesses who want a senior strategic sounding board without the cost or complexity of adding headcount.
advisory.whoForPara2: Often chosen by clients who have completed an Implementation engagement and want to sustain the discipline of structured strategic thinking.
advisory.whoForNote: Retainer engagements are limited to 6 clients per partner. We maintain small client lists intentionally — so the counsel you receive reflects genuine attention, not template thinking.
includes: Monthly 3-hour strategy session with a senior Meridian partner
Priority initiatives review and accountability tracking
Quarterly performance review against stated objectives
advisory.ctaSub: Reach out to discuss whether a retainer spot is currently available.

servicesIndex.headline: Three ways to work together.

services.title: Diagnostic Sprint
services.ideal: Businesses that sense something is wrong but can't locate the root cause.
deliverables: Full operational mapping across all functions
Financial leak analysis and margin reconstruction

services.title: Implementation
services.ideal: Businesses with a clear diagnosis that need hands-on help executing the changes.

services.title: Embedded Advisory
services.ideal: Leadership teams that want a senior strategic thinking partner without adding headcount.
deliverables: Monthly strategy sessions with senior partner

servicesIndex.ctaHeadline: Not sure which is right for you?
servicesIndex.ctaSub: A 30-minute call is usually enough to know.

industriesIndex.headline: Sector depth, not sector breadth.
industriesIndex.sub: We work exclusively in three sectors. That focus means we walk into every engagement already knowing the benchmarks, the common failure modes, and the levers that move the numbers.

industries.title: E-Commerce
industries.challenge: Peak volume management, omnichannel inventory, and returns processing for DTC and marketplace brands.

industries.title: Wholesale & Distribution
industries.challenge: Margin compression from rising logistics costs and fragmented supplier bases.

industries.title: Manufacturing
industries.challenge: Capacity underutilisation and overhead structures misaligned with volume realities.

industriesIndex.ctaSub: We occasionally work outside these verticals for the right client. Reach out and we'll be direct about fit.

wholesale.headline: Defending margin in a distribution business.
wholesale.sub: Wholesale distribution is a margin management business disguised as a logistics business. Our work in this sector focuses on the structural levers that protect and expand distribution margins.
wholesale.challengesHeadline: The four challenges we see most often

challenges.title: Supplier fragmentation
challenges.body: Too many suppliers, too little negotiating leverage. As SKU counts climb, purchasing power per supplier declines and administrative overhead rises disproportionately.

challenges.title: Logistics cost creep
challenges.body: Rising freight, fuel, and labour costs eat into distribution margins that were thin to begin with. Route inefficiencies compound over time without structured analysis.

challenges.title: Working capital trap
challenges.body: Slow-moving inventory ties up capital that could fund growth. Most distributors don't know which SKUs are actually profitable when carrying costs are included.

challenges.title: Customer concentration risk
challenges.body: Revenue is often concentrated in a handful of accounts. Losing one large customer can trigger a structural crisis rather than a recoverable shortfall.

wholesale.resultsPara1: Most wholesale distributors we work with have grown by adding volume without scrutinising whether that volume is actually profitable. Our diagnostic phase typically uncovers 2–4 percentage points of hidden margin within the existing business.
wholesale.resultsPara2: Implementation work then focuses on locking those gains in — through supplier consolidation, route optimisation, SKU discipline, and working capital management.

metrics.desc: Return on engagement fee within 12 months

wholesale.ctaSub: 30 days. A clear picture of where you are losing margin.

manufacturing.headline: More throughput from the assets you already own.
manufacturing.sub: Manufacturing improvement doesn't always require capital expenditure. The most durable gains come from aligning your overhead structure, scheduling discipline, and supplier relationships with actual volume realities.
manufacturing.challengesHeadline: The four challenges we see most often

challenges.title: Capacity vs. overhead mismatch
challenges.body: Fixed overhead structures built for peak volumes become a drag during contraction. Most manufacturers don't have a clear view of their true breakeven by product line.

challenges.title: Production scheduling inefficiency
challenges.body: Suboptimal scheduling creates unnecessary changeovers, inflated WIP, and missed delivery windows — all without appearing in the P&L as a named line item.

challenges.title: Supply chain concentration
challenges.body: Single-source dependencies create fragility. Many manufacturers have rationalised supplier counts too aggressively, trading resilience for short-term cost savings.

challenges.title: Make-vs-buy drift
challenges.body: Decisions made at one cost structure remain in place long after the economics have changed. Regular make-vs-buy review is rare but high-value.

manufacturing.resultsPara1: Our manufacturing engagements start with a detailed cost model — not the management accounts version, but a true product-level view with overhead properly allocated. That analysis typically surfaces 3–5 underperforming product lines that are consuming disproportionate capacity.
manufacturing.resultsPara2: Implementation then focuses on scheduling discipline, supplier consolidation, and — where warranted — rationalising the product range itself.

metrics.desc: Reduction in changeover time through scheduling

manufacturing.ctaSub: 30 days. A product-level view of where you are leaving margin on the floor.

items.title: Why operational problems are usually structural ones in disguise
items.excerpt: When leadership teams describe their business as 'too busy but not moving', they are almost always describing a structural design failure — not an effort problem.

items.title: The diagnostic before the prescription: why we never lead with solutions
items.excerpt: Most consultants arrive with answers. The honest ones arrive with questions. Understanding why changes everything about the quality of the recommendation that follows.

items.title: Margin compression in wholesale distribution: the three levers that actually move the number
items.excerpt: Wholesale distributors facing margin pressure typically look at procurement first. That is rarely where the biggest opportunity sits.

items.title: The counterintuitive case for selling fewer products
items.excerpt: Range expansion is the default growth strategy in retail. The data suggests it creates more complexity than revenue — and destroys margin in the process.

items.title: Embedded execution vs. advisory: which model actually changes behaviour
items.excerpt: The consulting industry has a delivery problem. Recommendations without implementation responsibility produce slide decks, not outcomes.

items.title: Improving manufacturing throughput without capital expenditure
items.excerpt: The instinct to solve capacity problems with new equipment is understandable. It is also frequently unnecessary. The scheduling and process lever is almost always larger.

philosophy.headline: The beliefs that shape how we work.
philosophy.openingLeft: Consulting has a reputation problem. Too many engagements produce reports that gather dust. We built Meridian because we believe there is a better model.
philosophy.openingRight1: The better model starts with a different set of beliefs. About what causes operational problems. About what clients deserve to hear. About what "done" actually means.
philosophy.openingRight2: These are the four beliefs we return to, in every engagement, when things get complicated.

principles.title: Problems have structural causes
principles.body: Every operational failure — a missed forecast, a margin leak, a broken handoff — is a symptom. The cause lives upstream in how the organisation is designed. We don't fix the symptom. We redesign the system that keeps producing it.

principles.title: Rigour over reassurance
principles.body: Most consultants tell clients what they want to hear. We tell clients what the data says. That means some engagements begin with uncomfortable findings. We believe discomfort at the diagnosis stage is far cheaper than comfort that delays the cure.

principles.title: Ownership transfers with delivery
principles.body: We measure success not by the quality of our slide decks but by whether the organisation can sustain the improvement after we leave. Every engagement ends with codified processes, trained teams, and measurable baselines — not a dependency.

principles.title: Depth over breadth
principles.body: We work in three industries. We could work in more. We choose not to because deep expertise — knowing the specific cost structures, compliance landscapes, and buyer behaviours of your sector — produces better outcomes than generalist pattern-matching.

philosophy.ctaSub: Curious whether our approach fits your situation?

approach.headline: A structured process designed to deliver, not just advise.
approach.sub: Three phases. Fixed timelines. Measurable outcomes at each stage. No open-ended retainers disguised as transformation programmes.

phases.title: Diagnostic
phases.outcome: A 30–50 page findings document and a ranked action roadmap, delivered in a structured readout with your leadership team.

phases.title: Implementation
steps: Progress reporting with financial impact tracking
phases.outcome: Measurable improvements across targeted metrics, with all changes embedded in your operating rhythm — not dependent on our continued presence.

phases.title: Handover
steps: Internal team training and knowledge transfer
Optional retainer structure for ongoing counsel
phases.outcome: Your team runs the improved system independently. We leave a capability, not a dependency.

principles.label: Fixed-fee engagements
principles.desc: No surprise billings. Scope, timeline, and price are agreed at the start.

principles.label: Diagnostic before prescription
principles.desc: We never recommend a solution before completing a structured assessment.

principles.label: No offshore delivery
principles.desc: The team you meet is the team that does the work. No hand-offs to junior associates.

approach.ctaSub: 30 days. A clear picture of where you are losing value.
